metadata.seed: I am fighting with my parents because I am 17 and have just began my first relationship and my parents have grounded me after finding out that I am dating before the age of 18

patient.query: I am fighting with my parents because I am 17 and have just began my first relationship and my parents have grounded me after finding out that I am dating before the age of 18

automatic_thoughts: they don't understand me
I'm being treated unfairly
I should be allowed to date

patient.query: Yeah, I guess I just feel like they don’t understand me at all. When we argue, it’s like they see me as this version of myself that I don't really relate to. I’m often just filled with this sense of hopelessness, thinking, “Will they ever get it?" It makes me want to just shut down.

automatic_thoughts: they don’t understand me
they see a version of myself I don't relate to
Will they ever get it?

patient.query: I think it’s when they assume I’m the same person I was a few years ago. They bring up my past mistakes or choices, and it’s like they can't see that I’ve grown or changed. It just feels suffocating, like I’m stuck in this box they’ve put me in, and it makes me feel really frustrated and even angry at times. I just... I want to scream, “I’m not that person anymore!”

I'm stuck in a box
I want to scream I'm not that person

My mind races with thoughts like, “Am I really that person?” and “Why can’t they just see me for who I am now?” It’s exhausting, honestly. I just want to feel accepted for who I am today.

patient.query: They’ll often bring it up during an argument or when I’m trying to talk about something important. Like, they'll say things like, “You’ve always had trouble with this,” or “Remember when you did that?” It feels like a punch to the gut. It’s like they're using my past as ammunition, and it just makes me feel so small. I can’t help but think, “Why can’t they let it go?” It just spirals from there.

automatic_thoughts: Why can't they let it go?
They're using my past as ammunition

I start questioning if I’ve really changed or if I'm just fooling myself. I just... I feel like I can’t win. It’s like, no matter how hard I try, I’m always going to be that “troubled” person in their eyes.

patient.query: I think it usually happens during family dinners or when I’m trying to share something important about my life. Like, I’ll start talking about a job I’m excited about or a new hobby, and suddenly, they’ll pivot to my past. It feels like they can’t even focus on the present. That shift just takes me back to old mistakes, and it’s like I can’t escape it. It makes me feel really small and anxious, like I’m just waiting for the next criticism to
